div.p: extermination of thousands of her citizens to go unpunished, thereby perpetrating a foul and corroding blot upon the fair fame of this great republic, the very thought of which would have caused the high-minded and patriotic framers of the Constitution of the United States to hide their faces with shame.1 Judge, you will aspire to the presidency of the United States; and if ever you turn your hand against me2 or the Latter-day Saints, you will feel the weight of the hand of the Almighty3 upon you; and you will live to see and know that I have testified the truth to you; for the conversation of this day will stick to you through life.
He [Judge Douglas] appeared very friendly, and acknowledged the truth and propriety of President Smith’s remarks. (May 18, 1843.) DHC 5:393–394.
I do not know when I shall have the privilege of speaking in a house large enough to convene the people. I find my lungs are failing with continual preaching in the open air to large assemblies.

I do not think there have been many good men4 on the earth since the days of Adam; but there was one good man and his name was Jesus.5 Many persons think a prophet must be a great deal better than anybody else.6 Suppose I would condescend—yes, I will call it condescend, to be a great deal better than any of you, I would be raised up to the highest heaven; and who should I have to accompany me?
I love that man better who swears7 a stream as long as my arm yet deals justice to his neighbors8 and mercifully deals his substance to the poor,9 than the long, smooth-faced hypocrite.10
I do not want you to think that I am very righteous, for I am not,11 God judges men according to the use they make of the light which He gives them.12

“We have a more sure word of prophecy, whereunto you do well to take heed, as unto a light that shineth in a dark place. We were eyewitnesses of his majesty and heard the voice of his excellent glory.”13 And what could be more sure? When He was transfigured on the mount,14 what could be more sure to them? Divines have been quarreling for ages about the meaning of this.
